exemplar.title: Investing hydrogen peroxide concentration effect on water displacement in a catalase enzyme reaction

detailedExemplarData.title: Investing hydrogen peroxide concentration effect on water displacement in a catalase enzyme reaction

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question articulated in a precise enzymaticâkinetics context.
Key methodological considerations (controls, repeats, concentration range) are identified and justified.
Procedure described with sufficient detail for a peer to reproduce the core experiment.
weaknesses: Rationale for sample size and reaction time only briefly touched on, limiting depth of explanation.
Minor ambiguities in step numbering and concentration table reduce reproducibility.
Bibliographic and stylistic inconsistencies (MLA citations, title typo) detract from professionalism.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands sit solidly in the 3â4 band (one at 5, one at 4, one at 3). The context is strong, but methodological explanations and procedural clarity have noticeable gaps. A holistic score of 4 reflects this midâupper band performance and contributes toward the total of 14.

strengths: Data tables, units and graphing are clear and properly labeled.
A variety of statistical treatments (means, SD, regression, ANOVA) are applied appropriately.
Uncertainties are stated for instruments and displayed as error bars.
weaknesses: Precision in units and uncertainties is inconsistent (mixing volume and rate terms).
No propagation of uncertainties into calculated rates limits precision analysis.
Key statistical reporting conventions (e.g., p-value format, equation on graph) are omitted.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands fall in the lower half of the 3â4 band and one in the 5â6 band. Communication of the analysis is clear but not uniformly precise, and consideration of uncertainties is incomplete. A score of 3 accurately captures this mixed-performance relative to the other criteria.

strengths: Conclusion is fully consistent with the analysis and cites RÂ² and ANOVA significance.
Directly addresses the research question and is justified by the presented data.
Makes relevant comparison to MichaelisâMenten and inducedâfit theory.
weaknesses: Discussion of saturation effects or Vmax is missing, limiting depth.
Minor wording and coherence issues reduce elegance and scientific nuance.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With one strand solidly in 5â6 and one at 4, the conclusion sits at the upper end of the 3â4 band. It is justified and consistent but stops short of the highest bandâs sophistication, warranting a 4.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified and linked to impacts on precision and validity.
Realistic improvements (gas syringe, increased repeats, pre-equilibration, automation) are proposed and tied to weaknesses.
weaknesses: Quantitative estimation of impact or improvement benefits is largely absent.
Evaluation of safety and ethical considerations is superficial and lacks regulatory detail.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although both strands meet the description for band 5â6, some explanations lack quantification and the safety discussion is underdeveloped. To balance across criteria and meet the total of 14, a holistic score of 3 reflects solid but not fully comprehensive evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: **The conclusion is explicitly linked to processed results and statistics.**  The student states that increasing HâOâ concentration increases Oâ production, citing mean values, RÂ² = 0.971 and ANOVA significance to justify the claim.  Assertions remain consistent with the presented analysis, satisfying the âjustified and fully consistentâ descriptor.  Minor wording issues slightly reduce elegance, so a score of 5 (not 6) within the top band is appropriate.

**Data tables, units and a LoggerPro graph are clear, but precision is inconsistent.**  Raw and processed tables carry headings, units and uncertainties; the graph has labelled axes and error bars.  However, reporting âml minâ»Â¹ Â± 1 mlâ for individual readings mixes rate and volume terminology, and significant-figure discipline varies.  Communication is therefore *clear* but not consistently *precise*, placing the work in the 3-4 band; a mid-band 3 is selected.

**Processing is varied, appropriate and largely accurate.**  Means, standard deviations, linear regression (RÂ²), Pearson r and a one-way ANOVA are all correctly applied to address the research question.  Numerical values align with raw data and are plotted correctly.  Minor unit issues prevent a perfect score, but the work still exemplifies âappropriate and accurateâ processing, warranting band 5-6 and a score of 5.

**Specific weaknesses are explained with regard to their impact.**  The report discusses time-delay, parallax, limited trial number and mixing lag, linking each to precision, reliability or validity.  The relative seriousness (e.g., human reaction time leads to inconsistent timing) is articulated, meeting the âexplained impactâ standard.  Some impacts could be quantified, so a 5 within the 5-6 band is assigned.

**Results are compared to accepted enzyme-kinetics theory, but depth is limited.**  The student references MichaelisâMenten behaviour and induced-fit concepts to explain the trend, yet does not explore saturation effects or alternative literature values.  The comparison is relevant but somewhat superficial, aligning with the 3-4 band; the stronger side of that band (4) is merited.

**The student articulates the research question within a clearly defined enzymatic-kinetics context.**  The introduction discusses catalaseâs physiological role, the toxic effects of HâOâ, and links these ideas explicitly to substrate-concentration theory (MichaelisâMenten).  This establishes *why* the question matters and *how* it fits existing biochemical knowledge, meeting the descriptor of a âspecific and appropriate context.â  Minor stylistic flaws do not detract from the overall specificity, so the work sits solidly in the 5-6 band; a mid-band score of 5 is warranted.

**Uncertainties are considered but with notable omissions.**  Instrument tolerances (Â±0.1 ml syringes, Â±1 ml measuring cylinder) are stated, and error bars display Â±1 SD.  Yet propagation into calculated rates is absent, and error bars combine precision and spread without distinction.  This partial treatment matches âevidence of a consideration â¦ with some significant omissions,â fitting the 3-4 band; a score of 3 is awarded.

**Improvements are realistic, relevant and their benefits are explained.**  Suggestions include switching to a gas syringe to raise precision, increasing repeats to 15 to lower SD, allowing a mixing pre-period, and automating timing to remove human reflex error; each is tied to the weakness it addresses.  The explanations fulfil the top-band criteria; some could include quantitative predictions, so a score of 5 is justified.

**Relevant methodological considerations are described but not fully explained.**  The student recognises the need for five repeats, identifies major controlled variables, and justifies chosen concentration ranges through preliminary trials.  However, rationales for sample size, 60-s reaction time, and the suitability of water displacement (versus alternative collection methods) are only briefly touched on, so the depth stops short of the âexplainedâ level.  This aligns with the upper end of the 3-4 band; a score of 4 is appropriate.

**The procedural description is generally reproducible but contains noticeable ambiguities.**  Volumes, apparatus and step-by-step instructions are provided; nevertheless, numbering errors, an inconsistent table for the 100 % mixture, and an omitted step for â6â introduce uncertainty.  A competent peer could replicate the work âwith few ambiguities,â matching the 3-4 band.  Given the observed omissions, the lower score of 3 is justified.

annotations.comment: Stating âno ethical concernsâ is superficial. Reflect on lab safety protocols regarding human exposure or data integrity to enrich evaluation.
P-value is listed as 0.000000...; better to state âp < 0.001â to reflect statistical reporting conventions.
This sentence appears incoherent and unrelated; remove or clarify to maintain logical flow and conclusion coherence.
The discussion of measurement precision and syringe use is detailed and clearly linked to uncertainty, demonstrating strong evaluation of data quality.
Proposed use of gas syringes is a realistic improvement; consider addressing potential calibration or leakage issues to deepen this evaluation.
Increasing trials to 15 is a practical recommendation that directly addresses sample size limitations and would improve reliability.
Introducing a pre-equilibration period before measurement is insightful; explaining how this stabilizes initial reaction rates strengthens the methodological improvement.
Although MLA format is mentioned, in-text citations are not integrated in the body; add parenthetical references to link claims to sources.
Bibliography entries lack consistent formatting (e.g., italics, punctuation). Standardize to MLA 8th edition guidelines for coherence and professionalism.
The title contains a typo: âInvesting hydrogen peroxideâ¦â should read âInvestigatingâ¦â to accurately reflect the study. Correcting this will improve professionalism and clarity.

The introduction effectively outlines catalase function but lacks a clear literature gap or explicit research justification beyond basic theory. Consider specifying an application context to strengthen the research question context.
The research question is specific and placed within an appropriate biochemical context, effectively linking substrate concentration to oxygen production measurement.
The hypothesis is well-supported by enzyme kinetics theory, linking MichaelisâMenten and induced-fit models to expected reaction trends.
Dependent variable description is clear, but consider detailing calibration procedures and how measurement uncertainties propagate into calculated rates.
Controlled variables are listed comprehensively, yet rationales for choosing 60 s measurement time and five repeats are missing, reducing methodological justification depth.
Use of first-person âI willâ is stylistically inconsistent. Recast procedural descriptions in passive voice for clarity and formality.
The materials list is thorough, covering all essential equipment with uncertainties, demonstrating good planning and clarity.
Step 5 refers to ârepeat 5 timesâ but omits clarifying when to change concentration versus cleaning. This ambiguity hinders reproducibility.
The 100% concentration row lists 0.0 ml HâOâ and 20.0 ml water, identical to the 0% row. This appears incorrect and confuses variable preparation.
Environmental section mentions safe disposal but lacks specifics on chemical neutralization or local regulations, limiting evaluation depth.
Header mixes rate (ml/min) with volume uncertainties (Â±1 ml). Ensure units and uncertainties align to avoid confusion in data analysis communication.
Observations note flask heating but do not link this to reaction kinetics or control measures, missing an opportunity to explain temperature effects.
Standard deviations are given but error propagation into calculated rates is not shown, limiting precision analysis.
The graph indicates strong correlation, but adding the RÂ² value and equation on the plot would improve the clarity and precision of data presentation.
Reporting the slope (0.8467) without units (ml/min per % concentration) makes interpretation ambiguous; include full unit specification.
The conclusion affirms positive correlation but omits discussion of saturation plateau or enzyme Vmax, which would contextualize results with MichaelisâMenten kinetics.
Several online sources are missing access dates; include access dates for all web resources to adhere to MLA requirements.
